action: Replace mutable-directory queue reconciliation in CI with exact-commit repository discovery, fail-closed validation, and accepted-artifact-only publication.

observed: The existing workflow still called effective-queue-reconciler.mjs and generate-effective-queue.mjs over mutable directory inputs.

The replacement workflow verifies the checked-out event commit, runs the current gate and discovery tests, derives the corpus from HEAD through repository object reads, and publishes only accepted output.
inferred: CI-generated effective queue artifacts are now structurally bound to one immutable repository state.
proposed: Make accepted effective-queue artifacts a required prerequisite for autonomous queue selection.
superseded: Mutable-directory CI reconciliation as authoritative queue evidence.
Uploading queue output regardless of acceptance state.
unresolved: The workflow has not yet produced a GitHub Actions run for this commit.
Autonomous team startup does not yet download and verify the accepted artifact.

verification: Workflow structure was committed and inspected; no workflow run was associated with the commit at recording time, so runtime success is not claimed.
privacy_boundary: Public repository metadata, abstract operational state, and generated artifacts only; private chat, health material, credentials, personal identifiers, and unreviewed uploads excluded.
handoff: Continuity Mining should next add a consumer-side startup verifier that accepts only an effective-queue artifact whose source commit matches the checked-out commit and whose manifest/blob identities remain intact.
